items.purpose: Train the estimator on standard inputs and labels.
Predicts labels or values for input samples using the trained estimator.
Returns probability estimates for each class on input samples.
Transforms input data using the fitted estimator or preprocessing pipeline.
Fits the estimator to the data and then transforms the input samples.
Returns the mean accuracy or R-squared score on the given test data.
Retrieves the current parameter names and values of the estimator.
Standardizes features by removing the mean and scaling to unit variance.
Scales each feature to a given range typically between zero and one.
Encodes target labels with values between zero and n_classes minus one.
Encodes categorical features as a one-hot numeric array.
Splits arrays into random train and test subsets.
Imputes missing values using a constant or statistical strategy.
Fits a logistic regression model for classification tasks.
Fits an ensemble of decision trees for classification tasks.
Fits a support vector classifier with a kernel function.
Partitions data into k clusters using the Lloyd algorithm.
Fits a linear model to minimize residual sum of squares.
Fits a single decision tree classifier to the data.
Chains multiple estimators into a single composite estimator.
Constructs a pipeline from given estimators using generated names.
Applies different transformers to different columns of a DataFrame.
Concatenates the results of multiple transformer objects.
Constructs a transformer from an arbitrary callable function.
Fits all transformers and the final estimator on the data.
Computes the fraction of correctly classified samples.
Computes the F1 score combining precision and recall.
Builds a text report showing classification metrics per class.
Computes a matrix of true versus predicted class labels.
Computes the area under the receiver operating characteristic curve.
Computes the mean squared error between true and predicted values.
Evaluates a score by cross-validation on a dataset.
Exhaustively searches over a parameter grid for the best estimator.
Searches over a random subset of the parameter space.
Evaluates metrics by cross-validation and returns multiple scores.
Provides train-test indices to preserve class distribution in each fold.
Generates all combinations of parameter values from a grid.
